title: Today's AI Brief: Anthropic's Cowork and CNN's Perplexity Lawsuit
summary: Anthropic launches Cowork, a Claude Desktop agent for non-technical users, while CNN sues Perplexity over 'verbatim' copycat articles.
imageAlt: Image from source article for Anthropic launches Cowork, a Claude Desktop agent that works in your files — no coding required
imageCaption: Anthropic launches Cowork, a Claude Desktop agent that works in your files — no coding required
imageSourceTitle: Anthropic launches Cowork, a Claude Desktop agent that works in your files — no coding required

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI Brief. We're looking at some interesting developments in the world of artificial intelligence. Anthropic has just launched Cowork, a new AI agent capability that brings the power of its Claude Code tool to non-technical users. This means you can now work with AI directly in your files without needing to know how to code. I'm pretty excited about this one - it's been a while coming.

According to Anthropic, the team built Cowork to make it easy for anyone to use AI in their daily work. This is a big deal because it opens up AI to a much wider audience. No more coding required - just work directly with your files and let the AI agent do the rest. I'm looking at this as a significant step towards making AI more accessible and user-friendly. Think of all the non-techies who can now play with AI without getting overwhelmed.

Anthropic launches Cowork, a Claude Desktop agent that works in your files — no coding required is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: Anthropic released Cowork on Monday, a new AI agent capability that extends the power of its wildly successful Claude Code tool to non-technical users — and according to company insiders, the team built the entire feature in approximately a week and a half, largely using Claude Code itself. The launch marks a major inflection point in the race to deliver pr... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

CNN sues Perplexity over ‘verbatim’ copycat articles, from theverge.com, is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: AI News Policy CNN sues Perplexity over ‘verbatim’ copycat articles Perplexity is accused of scraping CNN’s work without permission and providing users with content locked behind its paywall. The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
So that is the brief version: a few headlines, a little context, and just enough connective tissue to make the day easier to follow without turning the whole thing into homework.
